E.Value:
\r \r \r \r 返回\r \r \r

\r
\r
\r
\r
\r
\r
\r
\r
\r
\r
\r
\r It’s the end of the credits.\r
\r
\r
\r
\r
\r
\r
\r
\r
\r Really.\r
\r
\r
\r
\r
\r
\r
\r
\r
\r Omg.. this forever lasting text credits joke is so old.\r
\r
\r
\r
\r
\r
\r
\r
\r
\r Well humor is based in repetition.\r
\r
\r
\r
\r
\r
\r
\r
\r
\r So let’s play with it then.\r
\r
\r
\r
\r
\r
\r
\r
\r
\r Let’s use this space as education then.\r
\r
\r
\r Let’s start from basics.\r
\r Mass and inertia is the same thing.\r
\r
\r
\r That was easy!\r
\r
\r
\r
\r
\r
\r
\r
\r Ok then bit more advanced stuff.\r
\r
\r
\r Planck's constant is a physical constant used to describe the sizes of quanta in quantum mechanics. It is the proportionality constant between energy (E) of a photon and the frequency of its associated electromagnetic wave (v).\r
\r
\r
\r That’s it.\r
\r
\r
\r Bye!!\r
\r
\r
\r
\r
\r
\r
